description: Replace hardcoded Thompson Sampling suggestions with a classify → predict → LLM pipeline. Add ActivityClassifier (8 semantic event categories) and WorkflowStatePredictor (flow state probability distribution). Remove all hardcoded suggestion actions and text. System starts rule-based and upgrades to ML as data accumulates.

userStories.title: Create ActivityClassifier with rule-based cold start
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml developer, I need an ActivityClassifier model that categorizes raw events into semantic activity categories so that downstream models and the LLM understand what the user is doing.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/sigil_ml/models/activity.py with ActivityClassifier class
CATEGORIES constant defines 8 categories: creating, refining, verifying, navigating, researching, integrating, communicating, idle
At cold start, both creating and refining map to a single 'editing' category (split later when ML trains)
classify(event: dict) -> dict returns {category: str, confidence: float, method: 'rules'|'ml'}
classify_batch(events: list[dict]) -> list[dict] classifies a buffer of events
Rule-based classification covers all 6 event kinds: file, process, hyprland, git, terminal, ai
is_trained property returns False for rule-based, True after ML training
Weight persistence via joblib at weights_path('activity')
pytest tests/test_models.py passes with new ActivityClassifier tests
userStories.notes: Follow the model pattern from stuck.py: FEATURE_NAMES constant, __init__ with weight loading, predict method, train method, is_trained property, _save method. For rule-based classification: kind=file → editing, kind=terminal + test/build cmd → verifying, kind=git → integrating, kind=ai → researching, kind=hyprland → navigating, kind=process → navigating. Use event.IsTestOrBuildCmd logic (check for go test, pytest, npm test, make, cargo test, etc.) to identify verifying terminal commands.

userStories.title: Add activity feature extraction for ML training
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml developer, I need feature extraction for the ActivityClassifier so that ML training has proper input features derived from event kind and payload.
acceptanceCriteria: New function extract_activity_features(event: dict) -> dict in features.py
Features extracted from event kind, source, and payload fields
Features include: event kind one-hot (6 floats), payload key presence flags, command type classification for terminal events, file extension category for file events
Returns a flat dict of floats suitable for sklearn input
pytest tests/test_features.py passes with new tests
userStories.notes: Keep it simple. One-hot encode event.kind (file=1,0,0,0,0,0 etc). For terminal events, check if payload.cmd contains test/build keywords. For file events, extract extension from payload.path and categorize (.py, .go, .js → code, .md, .txt → docs, .json, .toml, .yaml → config). Include a has_exit_code flag and exit_code_nonzero flag from payload.

userStories.title: Create WorkflowStatePredictor with rule-based cold start
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml developer, I need a WorkflowStatePredictor that assesses the user's current workflow state from classified events, replacing the hardcoded SuggestionPolicy.
acceptanceCriteria: New file src/sigil_ml/models/workflow.py with WorkflowStatePredictor class
FLOW_STATES constant: deep_work, shallow_work, exploring, blocked, winding_down
predict(classified_events: list[dict], session_info: dict) -> dict returns full state assessment

State assessment includes: flow_state (dict of probabilities summing to 1.0), dominant_state, momentum (-1 to 1), focus_score (0-1), dominant_activity, activity_distribution, session_elapsed_min, method, confidence

Rule-based prediction: high editing+low navigating → deep_work, high verifying+failures → blocked, high navigating+researching → exploring, high integrating+decreasing velocity → winding_down, default → shallow_work
Momentum computed by comparing event count in recent half vs older half of window
Focus score computed as inverse of Shannon entropy over activity category distribution normalized to 0-1
is_trained property returns False for rule-based, True after ML training
Weight persistence via joblib at weights_path('workflow')
pytest tests/test_models.py passes with new WorkflowStatePredictor tests
userStories.notes: For Shannon entropy: H = -sum(p * log2(p)) for each category where p > 0. Max entropy = log2(num_categories). Focus score = 1 - (H / max_H). session_info dict has keys: session_elapsed_min (float), task_phase (str or None), test_failures (int). For momentum: split classified_events in half by index, count events in each half, momentum = (recent_count - older_count) / max(total_count, 1), clamp to [-1, 1].

userStories.title: Add workflow state feature extraction for ML training
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml developer, I need feature extraction for the WorkflowStatePredictor so that ML training has proper window-level features.
acceptanceCriteria: New function extract_workflow_features(classified_events: list[dict], session_info: dict) -> dict in features.py
Features include: 8 normalized category counts, category entropy, event rate (events/min), category transition count, time in dominant category fraction, recent bias weight
session_info dict includes session_elapsed_min, task_phase, test_failures
Returns flat dict of floats suitable for sklearn input
pytest tests/test_features.py passes with new tests
userStories.notes: Category counts: count events per category, divide by total events. Category transitions: count how many times consecutive events have different categories. Recent bias: weight last 25% of events 2x in category counts. Event rate: len(events) / max(time_span_minutes, 1/60).

userStories.title: Add synthetic data generators for new models
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml developer, I need synthetic data generators for the new models to support cold-start ML training.
acceptanceCriteria: generate_activity_data(n=500) added to training/synthetic.py
Generates events with realistic kind/payload distributions and corresponding category labels
generate_workflow_data(n=200) added to training/synthetic.py
Generates classified-event windows with realistic activity distributions and flow state labels
Both add Gaussian noise and shuffle before returning
pytest tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Follow the pattern from generate_stuck_data() and generate_duration_data() in the same file. For activity data: generate events with random kind values and payloads, assign categories based on rules. For workflow data: generate windows (lists of category counts) with labels — deep_work windows have high editing, low navigating; blocked windows have high verifying; exploring windows have high navigating+researching.

userStories.title: Remove hardcoded suggestion strings from QualityEstimator
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml developer, I want to remove hardcoded suggestion text from the QualityEstimator so that suggestion generation is entirely the LLM's responsibility.
acceptanceCriteria: _suggest_for_degraded() function deleted from quality.py (currently at line 196)
suggestion key removed from predict() return dict in quality.py
QualityResponse in server.py no longer includes suggestion field
All quality scoring logic preserved unchanged — only suggestion text removed
grep -r '_suggest_for_degraded' src/ returns no results
pytest tests/ passes with updated assertions
userStories.notes: In quality.py predict() method, find where suggestion is added to the return dict and remove it. Also remove the call to _suggest_for_degraded(). In server.py find QualityResponse and remove the suggestion: str | None field. Update test assertions that check for suggestion key in quality prediction output.

userStories.title: Integrate ActivityClassifier into poller event processing
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml user, I want every event classified as it enters the poller buffer so that downstream predictions have semantic activity context.
acceptanceCriteria: Poller constructor accepts models['activity'] (ActivityClassifier instance)
Each event is classified as it enters the buffer in _poll_once()
Classified events stored in buffer as {...event, '_category': str, '_category_confidence': float}
Activity summary prediction written to ml_predictions with model='activity' on each prediction cycle
Activity prediction format: {window_summary: {category: count}, recent: [{ts, kind, category}], dominant: str, method: str, confidence: float}
recent array capped at last 10 events for token efficiency
pytest tests/ passes
userStories.notes: In poller.py __init__, add self.activity = models['activity']. In _poll_once(), after appending event to buffer, call self.activity.classify(event) and add _category and _category_confidence keys to the event dict in the buffer. In _predict_and_write(), build the activity summary from self._buffer and write it using self._write(conn, 'activity', summary, confidence, PREDICTION_TTL_SEC).

userStories.title: Replace suggest prediction in poller with workflow state
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml user, I want the poller to write workflow state assessments instead of hardcoded suggestion actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Poller constructor accepts models['workflow'] (WorkflowStatePredictor) instead of models['suggest'] (SuggestionPolicy)
_predict_and_write() calls self.workflow.predict() instead of self.suggest.predict()
Prediction written to ml_predictions with model='suggest' (preserved name for Go compatibility)
New prediction format includes flow_state dict, dominant_state, momentum, focus_score, dominant_activity, activity_distribution, session_elapsed_min, method, confidence
_FALLBACK_SUGGEST updated to new format with shallow_work as default state and neutral momentum/focus
Old extract_suggest_features() call removed from poller
pytest tests/ passes
userStories.notes: In poller.py, replace self.suggest with self.workflow. Build session_info dict from task data: session_elapsed_min from task started_at, task_phase from task phase, test_failures from task test_fails. Pass classified buffer events (with _category tags from US-007) and session_info to self.workflow.predict(). Write result to model='suggest'. Update _FALLBACK_SUGGEST to: {flow_state: {deep_work: 0, shallow_work: 1.0, exploring: 0, blocked: 0, winding_down: 0}, dominant_state: 'shallow_work', momentum: 0.0, focus_score: 0.5, dominant_activity: 'idle', activity_distribution: {}, session_elapsed_min: 0, method: 'rules', confidence: 0.5}.

userStories.title: Delete SuggestionPolicy and related code
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml developer, I want to remove all Thompson Sampling bandit code and hardcoded action strings so the codebase reflects the new architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: src/sigil_ml/models/suggest.py file deleted
ACTIONS list no longer exists anywhere in codebase
extract_suggest_features() function removed from features.py
All references to SuggestionPolicy removed from server.py, poller.py, training/trainer.py
grep -r 'suggest_commit|suggest_break|suggest_step_back|ACTIONS|SuggestionPolicy' src/ returns no results
pytest tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Delete src/sigil_ml/models/suggest.py entirely. In features.py remove the extract_suggest_features() function (lines 128-187) and its phase list. In training/trainer.py remove _train_suggest() method and its call from train_all(). In server.py remove SuggestRequest/SuggestResponse classes and old suggest endpoint imports. In test files remove any tests that directly test SuggestionPolicy. Make sure no import of suggest or SuggestionPolicy remains.

userStories.title: Add /predict/activity API endpoint
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml API consumer, I want a /predict/activity endpoint so I can query event classifications on demand.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /predict/activity endpoint added to server.py
Request accepts optional events list or uses current poller buffer
Response returns activity summary: window_summary, recent, dominant, method, confidence
ActivityClassifier added to /health endpoint model status reporting
pytest tests/test_server.py passes with new endpoint tests
userStories.notes: Add ActivityRequest (events: list[dict] | None = None) and ActivityResponse (window_summary: dict, recent: list, dominant: str, method: str, confidence: float) Pydantic models. In the endpoint handler, if events provided classify them directly, otherwise return latest activity prediction from ml_predictions table. Add 'activity' to the models dict checked in /health.

userStories.title: Replace /predict/suggest endpoint with workflow state response
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml API consumer, I want the /predict/suggest endpoint to return workflow state assessments in the new format.
acceptanceCriteria: /predict/suggest endpoint returns new schema: flow_state, dominant_state, momentum, focus_score, dominant_activity, activity_distribution, session_elapsed_min, method, confidence
Old SuggestRequest/SuggestResponse replaced with WorkflowStateRequest/WorkflowStateResponse Pydantic models
Request accepts optional task_id or pre-computed classified_events
Breaking change: old action+confidence format no longer returned
pytest tests/test_server.py passes with updated tests
userStories.notes: WorkflowStateRequest(task_id: str | None = None, classified_events: list[dict] | None = None). WorkflowStateResponse(flow_state: dict[str, float], dominant_state: str, momentum: float, focus_score: float, dominant_activity: str, activity_distribution: dict[str, float], session_elapsed_min: float, method: str, confidence: float). If task_id provided, extract classified events from buffer and session_info from task. Call workflow model predict().

userStories.title: Update server startup and model wiring
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml developer, I need the server startup to load and wire the new models correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: ActivityClassifier instantiated and loaded at startup alongside other models
WorkflowStatePredictor instantiated and loaded at startup alongside other models
Old SuggestionPolicy import and instantiation removed
Models dict passed to poller includes 'activity' and 'workflow' keys instead of 'suggest'
/health endpoint reports status of all models including activity and workflow
sigil-ml serve starts without errors
pytest tests/ passes
userStories.notes: In server.py startup (around line 55-93), replace: from sigil_ml.models.suggest import SuggestionPolicy → from sigil_ml.models.activity import ActivityClassifier and from sigil_ml.models.workflow import WorkflowStatePredictor. Replace _suggest = SuggestionPolicy() with _activity = ActivityClassifier() and _workflow = WorkflowStatePredictor(). Update models dict passed to EventPoller: replace 'suggest': _suggest with 'activity': _activity, 'workflow': _workflow. Update /health to report _activity and _workflow status.

userStories.title: Add ML training path for ActivityClassifier
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml developer, I need ML training for the ActivityClassifier so it upgrades from rules to learned classification after sufficient data.
acceptanceCriteria: ActivityClassifier.train(X, y) fits an SGDClassifier with partial_fit()
partial_fit() used for incremental learning (no full retrain needed)
New method _train_activity() added to training/trainer.py
Training collects events from events table and applies rule-based labels as initial training data
Reads ml_correction events (kind='ml_correction') for high-signal label overrides
Minimum 500 events required before first ML training
After training, classify() uses ML model instead of rules, with method='ml'
train_all() in trainer.py calls _train_activity()
pytest tests/ passes
userStories.notes: In activity.py add train(X, y) method that calls SGDClassifier(loss='log_loss').partial_fit(X, y, classes=CATEGORIES). The trainer._train_activity() should: query SELECT kind, payload FROM events ORDER BY id LIMIT 2000, extract features via extract_activity_features(), generate labels via rule-based classify(), then call activity.train(X, y). For ml_correction events: query SELECT payload FROM events WHERE kind='ml_correction', override the rule-based label with the correction.

userStories.title: Add ML training path for WorkflowStatePredictor
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml developer, I need ML training for the WorkflowStatePredictor so it upgrades from rules to learned state prediction after sufficient task data.
acceptanceCriteria: WorkflowStatePredictor.train(X, y) fits a GradientBoostingClassifier
New method _train_workflow() added to training/trainer.py
Training extracts classified-event windows from completed tasks
Labels derived from task outcomes: fast completion with few failures → deep_work, long with many failures → blocked phases
Reads ml_feedback events (kind='ml_feedback') to reinforce state predictions
Minimum 20 completed tasks required before first ML training, falls back to generate_workflow_data() synthetic data
train_all() in trainer.py calls _train_workflow()
pytest tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Follow the pattern from _train_stuck() in trainer.py. Query completed tasks, for each task extract events in the task window, classify them with ActivityClassifier, compute workflow features via extract_workflow_features(). Label logic: if (completed_at - started_at) < median_duration AND test_fails < 3 → deep_work, if test_fails > 5 AND (completed_at - started_at) > 2*median_duration → blocked, else → shallow_work. Use GradientBoostingClassifier(n_estimators=100, max_depth=3, learning_rate=0.1) matching stuck.py config.

userStories.title: Update training scheduler for new feedback signals
userStories.description: As a sigil-ml developer, I want the training scheduler to trigger retraining based on new feedback events, not just completed tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: Scheduler tracks ml_feedback event count since last retrain
Scheduler tracks ml_correction event count since last retrain
Activity classifier retraining triggers after 500 new events (separate from full retrain cycle)
Workflow retraining uses same trigger as stuck/duration (10 completed tasks, 1hr min interval)
Correction events trigger immediate activity retrain after 5 corrections
pytest tests/ passes
userStories.notes: In scheduler.py, add new counters: _last_event_count, _last_correction_count. In check_and_retrain(), query SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events to get total event count, and SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events WHERE kind='ml_correction' for corrections. If events since last check >= 500, trigger _train_activity() only. If corrections since last check >= 5, trigger _train_activity() immediately. Keep existing task-based trigger for full retrain (stuck + duration + workflow).
